decisions.title: AI interpretation run creates background task with notification
decisions.detail: Each AI interpretation run will create a single task that operates in the background within a task list, similar to the CC platform. A notification will be sent upon its completion.

evidence.quote: Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes

decisions.title: AI interpretation notification mechanism details pending
decisions.detail: The specific details regarding the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion are awaiting confirmation in the upcoming requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Two image sources for AI interpretation
decisions.detail: Images for AI interpretation can originate from two sources: either a large-scale, nationwide batch of imagery provided by Vendor A on a one-time basis, or individual images uploaded directly by the user.

evidence.quote: Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user

decisions.title: User confirmation and monitoring of suspected points
decisions.detail: After AI interpretation is complete and a list is generated, users can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch or monitoring.

evidence.quote: After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring

decisions.title: Monitoring suspected points requires reason and polygon upload
decisions.detail: When a user places a suspected point under monitoring, they must provide a reason and upload a polygon. Large-area polygons are valid, but overly small areas will be automatically filtered out.

evidence.quote: When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)

decisions.title: Duplicate image upload prevention for large files
decisions.detail: To manage large image file sizes, a mechanism is required to prevent images from being uploaded more than once.

evidence.quote: Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

decisions.title: Two-period image comparison will be manual annotation
decisions.detail: The two-period image comparison feature will no longer pull images from the image library; instead, users will annotate this manually.

evidence.quote: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead

decisions.title: On-site workstation for model execution
decisions.detail: A workstation will be deployed on-site. Images will be sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which will then call the workstation to run the model.

evidence.quote: A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model

decisions.title: Base map provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer
decisions.detail: The base map for the system will be sourced from GIS Provider B and served via GeoServer.

evidence.quote: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

decisions.title: Map viewer functionality for historical imagery and filtering
decisions.detail: The map viewer will be capable of loading historically uploaded imagery and will support filtering this imagery list by date and image-name keyword. It will also support filtering by image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone) and displaying points on the map.

evidence.quote: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

decisions.title: Map viewer 'locate' button for points
decisions.detail: When a point is opened in the map viewer, a 'locate' button will be available, which, when pressed, will automatically zoom the map to that specific location.

evidence.quote: Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location

decisions.title: Automatic imagery revert to point display on zoom out
decisions.detail: If the map is zoomed out beyond a specific scale, the displayed imagery will automatically revert to a point display.

evidence.quote: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display

decisions.title: Map view locked in comparison mode with release option
decisions.detail: In comparison mode, the map view will be locked, preventing panning. A 'release drag mode' button will be provided to restore panning functionality.

evidence.quote: In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning

decisions.title: User adjustable image transparency
decisions.detail: Users must be able to adjust image transparency, which is noted as an important feature for orthophoto analysis.

evidence.quote: Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)

decisions.title: Map viewer design references Forest Land Disaster Prevention System
decisions.detail: The design of the map viewer will reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.

evidence.quote: The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

decisions.title: Projects organized by year for suspected points
decisions.detail: Projects will be organized by year, and suspected points can be created within these yearly projects.

evidence.quote: Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

decisions.title: Suspected point layers by county/city pending confirmation
decisions.detail: A decision on whether to split suspected point layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Suspected points require polygon, no image upload
decisions.detail: Suspected points necessitate the upload of a polygon, but no image upload is required for them.

evidence.quote: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

decisions.title: Each year forms one suspected point layer
decisions.detail: For suspected point management, each year will constitute a distinct layer.

evidence.quote: Each year forms one layer

decisions.title: New Survey Point Data interface needed
decisions.detail: A 'New Survey Point Data' interface needs to be added. This interface should include fields for drawing number and key-monitoring-zone, referencing manual figure 2.13 for details.

evidence.quote: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

decisions.title: Legacy MIS file system migration and redesign
decisions.detail: The existing MS file management system (legacy MIS system) will be migrated to the new system. The interface will be redesigned, but its core functionality will remain unchanged.

evidence.quote: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

decisions.title: MIS migration driven by information-security compliance
decisions.detail: The primary motivation for migrating the legacy MIS system is to meet information-security compliance requirements.

evidence.quote: The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements

decisions.title: MIS migration details pending confirmation
decisions.detail: Further details regarding the migration of the legacy MIS system are awaiting confirmation in the requirements interview.

evidence.quote: Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

decisions.title: Legacy system has email-based follow-up/reminder feature
decisions.detail: The existing legacy system already includes a follow-up/reminder feature, which primarily uses email notifications.

evidence.quote: The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification

decisions.title: New system follow-up from suspected points
decisions.detail: For the new system's follow-up feature, items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module.

evidence.quote: Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module

decisions.title: New system follow-up schedule tracking interface
decisions.detail: The new system's follow-up feature will include a schedule-tracking interface, such as one showing days remaining.

evidence.quote: Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)

decisions.title: New system follow-up dispatch interface
decisions.detail: The new system will include a follow-up dispatch interface, for which Shao-Chien has already prepared a first draft.

evidence.quote: Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)

decisions.title: Follow-up connects to survey-report management
decisions.detail: After a follow-up is completed, it will connect to survey-report management, including recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.

evidence.quote: After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

decisions.title: A-Bao requests data-flow document for AI interpretation
decisions.detail: A-Bao has requested a data-flow document that explains how data moves after AI interpretation completion, specifically detailing data sourcing and computation locations.

evidence.quote: A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: [Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)]

decisions.title: Data flow scope: image source to monitoring database
decisions.detail: The data flow documentation will cover the path from the image source, through interpretation task execution (at the workstation), to result output, and finally to the monitoring database.

evidence.quote: Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database
evidence.approximate_location: Under heading: [Data Flow Documentation (requested by A-Bao)]

decisions.title: Project bidding process underway, expected mid-May kick-off
decisions.detail: The project is currently in the bidding process and is anticipated to kick off in mid-May if the bidding proceeds smoothly.

evidence.quote: The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May

decisions.title: Requirements interview and interface confirmation immediately post-bid award
decisions.detail: On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, there may be an immediate need for a requirements interview and interface confirmation.

evidence.quote: On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately

decisions.title: Sherry to finish 'add suspected point' data interface by 2026-05-18
decisions.detail: Sherry is tasked with completing the 'add suspected point' data interface, referencing figure 2.13, by May 18, 2026.

evidence.quote: Sherry | Finish the "add suspected point" data interface (refer to figure 2.13) | Before 5/18

evidence.quote: Sherry | Organize existing interfaces (including the parts Shao-Chien owns) for use in the requirements interview | Before 5/20

decisions.detail: Sherry needs to prepare the data-flow document, covering image source, compute node, and data direction, for A-Bao's review by May 18, 2026.

evidence.quote: Sherry | Prepare the data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao to review | Before 5/18

decisions.title: Next meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15 to discuss system architecture
decisions.detail: The next meeting is scheduled for May 15, 2026, with the planned topic of discussing system architecture.

evidence.quote: Planned topic: Discuss system architecture

decisions.title: First requirements interview scheduled for 2026-05-20
decisions.detail: The first requirements interview is scheduled for May 20, 2026.

evidence.quote: Planned topic: First requirements interview

decisions.detail: Preparation for the requirements interview includes consolidating existing interfaces.

decisions.title: Requirements interview prep: system architecture diagram
decisions.detail: Preparation for the requirements interview includes preparing a system architecture diagram.

evidence.quote: Prep needed: Consolidate existing interfaces, system architecture diagram, data flow diagram

decisions.detail: Preparation for the requirements interview includes preparing a data flow diagram.

evidence.quote: Prep needed: Consolidate existing interfaces, system architecture diagram, data flow diagram

decisions.title: Open question: AI interpretation notification mechanism details
decisions.detail: The specific details of how users will be notified upon completion of AI interpretation tasks remain an open question.

evidence.quote: Details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism

decisions.detail: It needs to be confirmed whether the suspected-point layer should be segmented by county/city.

decisions.detail: The specific design details for the interfaces involved in the legacy MIS system migration are still to be confirmed.

implementation_scope: Develop a background task system for AI interpretation runs, including task list management and completion notifications (similar to CC platform).
Implement image ingestion pipelines to support two sources for AI interpretation: large-scale batch upload from Vendor A and individual user uploads.
Build functionality for users to confirm suspected AI interpretation points (yes/no) and designate them for watch or monitoring.
Implement a mechanism for users to provide a reason and upload a polygon when placing a suspected point under monitoring, including automatic filtering of overly small polygons.
Develop a duplicate image upload prevention mechanism, especially for large image files.
Implement a manual annotation feature for two-period image comparison, without reliance on an image library.
Develop a map viewer capable of loading and displaying historically uploaded imagery.
Implement filtering functionality within the map viewer for imagery by date, image-name keyword, and image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone).
Implement point display functionality on the map viewer.
Add a 'locate' button in the map viewer to automatically zoom to a selected point's location.
Implement automatic imagery display reversion to point display when the map is zoomed out beyond a specific scale.
Implement a locked map view in comparison mode, preventing panning, with a 'release drag mode' button to re-enable panning.
Develop functionality for users to adjust image transparency in the map viewer.
Implement a project management structure that organizes projects by year for suspected points.
Develop suspected point creation functionality requiring polygon upload (without image upload).
Design and implement suspected point data layers, with each year forming a distinct layer.
Develop a 'New Survey Point Data' interface, including fields for drawing number and key-monitoring-zone (referencing manual figure 2.13).
Migrate the legacy MS file management system to the new platform, redesigning its interface while preserving core functionality.
Develop a new follow-up module that integrates with suspected points, allowing selection of items for follow-up.
Implement a schedule-tracking interface within the follow-up module (e.g., showing days remaining).
Develop a follow-up dispatch interface (based on an existing draft).
Integrate the follow-up completion process with survey-report management, capturing time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
Prepare a data-flow document detailing the process from image source, through AI interpretation task execution at the workstation, to result output and storage in the monitoring database.

constraints.text: The AI model execution must occur on an on-site workstation, with images sent to the client's Access Point (AP) which then calls the workstation.
The base map must be sourced from GIS Provider B and served via GeoServer.
The map viewer's design must reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System.
The migration of the legacy MIS system is mandated by information-security compliance requirements.
The project is expected to kick off in mid-May, dependent on the bidding process.
Immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation may be required on the afternoon of the bid award day.
The 'add suspected point' data interface, referencing figure 2.13, must be completed by 2026-05-18.
Existing interfaces (including those managed by Shao-Chien) must be organized for the requirements interview by 2026-05-20.
The data-flow document (image source, compute node, data direction) for A-Bao's review must be prepared by 2026-05-18.
A system architecture discussion meeting is scheduled for 2026-05-15.
The first requirements interview is scheduled for 2026-05-20.
Preparation for the requirements interview requires consolidating existing interfaces, preparing a system architecture diagram, and preparing a data flow diagram.

architectural_decisions.text: The legacy MS file management system will be migrated to the new system, with its interface redesigned but core functionality preserved.

open_technical_questions: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., push technology, polling interval, specific API endpoints)? (D002, D041)
Should suspected-point data layers be split by county/city? This impacts data modeling, storage, and retrieval strategies. (D017, D042)
What are the interface design details (e.g., API contracts, data types, communication protocols) for the migrated legacy MIS system? (D023, D043)
Who are the recipients of follow-up notifications and what is the technical implementation of their corresponding schedule (e.g., notification service logic, user role mapping)? (D044)

acceptance_criteria: Given an AI interpretation run is initiated, then a single task is created and displayed in the background task list.
Given an AI interpretation task completes, then a notification is sent to the user.
The system allows AI interpretation runs to be initiated using a large-scale, nationwide batch of imagery from Vendor A.
The system allows AI interpretation runs to be initiated using individual images uploaded directly by the user.
Given an AI interpretation run is complete and a list of suspected points is generated, when a user reviews a suspected point, then they can select "yes" or "no" to confirm the point.
Given a user confirms a suspected point, then they can choose to place it under "watch" or "monitoring".

Given a user chooses to place a suspected point under monitoring, when the user attempts to save the monitoring entry without providing a reason, then the system prevents saving and prompts for a reason.

Given a user chooses to place a suspected point under monitoring, when the user attempts to save the monitoring entry without uploading a polygon, then the system prevents saving and prompts for a polygon upload.
When a large-area polygon is uploaded for monitoring, then the system accepts it.
When an overly small-area polygon is uploaded for monitoring, then the system automatically filters it out.
When a user attempts to upload an image that is identical to an already uploaded image, then the system prevents the duplicate upload.
The two-period image comparison feature allows users to manually annotate images.
The two-period image comparison feature does NOT provide functionality to pull images from the image library.
The map viewer displays a list of historically uploaded imagery.
The map viewer allows filtering of historically uploaded imagery by date.
The map viewer allows filtering of historically uploaded imagery by image-name keyword.
The map viewer allows filtering of historically uploaded imagery by image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone).
The map viewer displays points on the map.
When a point is opened in the map viewer, then a 'locate' button is visible.
When the 'locate' button is pressed, then the map automatically zooms to the location of the selected point.
When the map is zoomed out beyond a specific, defined scale, then the displayed imagery automatically changes to a point display.
When the map viewer is in comparison mode, then users cannot pan the map.
When the map viewer is in comparison mode, then a "release drag mode" button is visible.
When the "release drag mode" button is pressed, then map panning functionality is restored.
The map viewer provides a control (e.g., slider) that allows users to adjust the transparency of displayed images.
Adjusting the image transparency control changes the transparency of the displayed image in real-time.
The system organizes projects by year.
Users can create suspected points within projects organized by year.
When creating a suspected point, users must upload a polygon.
When creating a suspected point, users are not required to upload an image.
For suspected point management, each year forms a distinct layer.
A "New Survey Point Data" interface is available.
The "New Survey Point Data" interface includes a field for "drawing number".
The "New Survey Point Data" interface includes a field for "key-monitoring-zone".
The core functionality of the legacy MIS file management system is preserved in the new system.
Users can select one or more suspected points to initiate a follow-up.
Selected suspected points can be successfully integrated into the follow-up module.
The follow-up module includes a schedule-tracking interface.
The schedule-tracking interface displays information such as "days remaining" for follow-up items.
A follow-up dispatch interface is available within the system.
After a follow-up is completed, the system records the completion time in survey-report management.
After a follow-up is completed, the system records a description of the follow-up in survey-report management.
After a follow-up is completed, the system records the action taken in survey-report management.
After a follow-up is completed, the system allows uploading and records images in survey-report management.
test_scenarios: Initiate an AI interpretation run with a user-uploaded image and verify a background task is created, runs, and completes with a notification.
Initiate an AI interpretation run with a large-scale batch of imagery from Vendor A and verify a background task is created, runs, and completes with a notification.
After an AI interpretation run, confirm a suspected point and choose to place it under 'Watch'.
After an AI interpretation run, confirm a suspected point and choose to place it under 'Monitoring', providing a valid reason and uploading a valid polygon.
Attempt to place a suspected point under monitoring without providing a reason and verify the system prevents saving and prompts for a reason.
Attempt to place a suspected point under monitoring without uploading a polygon and verify the system prevents saving and prompts for a polygon upload.
Upload a unique, large image file.
Attempt to upload the exact same image file again and verify the system prevents the duplicate upload.
Load the map viewer and verify historical imagery is displayed.
Filter historical imagery by date range, image-name keyword, and image type (satellite, aerial photo, drone) simultaneously, and verify the displayed list updates correctly.
Open a specific point on the map and click the 'locate' button, verifying the map automatically zooms to that point's location.
Zoom the map out beyond the defined threshold and verify that imagery automatically reverts to a point display.
Enter comparison mode, attempt to pan the map to verify it is locked, then click 'release drag mode' and verify panning functionality is restored.
Adjust the image transparency slider in the map viewer and verify the displayed image's transparency changes interactively.
Create a new suspected point within a project belonging to a specific year, uploading only a polygon, and verify it is created successfully within that year's layer.
Migrate existing MIS files to the new system and verify that core functionalities for managing these files remain unchanged.
Select multiple suspected points and initiate a new follow-up item, verifying they are integrated into the follow-up module.
Verify that the newly created follow-up item appears in the schedule-tracking interface with the correct 'days remaining'.
Complete a follow-up task, providing completion time, description, action taken, and uploading images, then verify all details are accurately recorded in the survey-report management system.
Verify the follow-up dispatch interface loads correctly.
edge_cases: Initiate multiple AI interpretation runs concurrently and observe task management and notification behavior.
Upload an image that is visually identical but has minor metadata differences; verify if it is still considered a duplicate by the system.
Attempt to upload a very large image file that exceeds potential system limits (if any are specified).
Attempt to place a suspected point under monitoring with an overly small polygon, verifying it is automatically filtered out.
Attempt to upload a polygon with zero area or a self-intersecting polygon for a suspected point, and verify system validation.
No historical imagery is available in the map viewer; verify the display and filtering options behave gracefully.
Rapidly zoom in and out around the threshold where imagery reverts to point display, ensuring smooth transitions.
Set image transparency to both minimum (fully opaque) and maximum (fully transparent) values.
Attempt to create a suspected point without uploading any polygon.
Attempt to select a non-existent or invalid suspected point for follow-up.
Enter an exceptionally long description or action taken text when completing a follow-up and verify it is handled correctly by survey-report management.
unresolved_behaviors: What are the specific details regarding the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion (e.g., in-app, email, push notification, content, dismissability)? (Owned by PM/QA)
Should suspected point layers be segmented (split) by county/city? (Owned by PM/QA)
What are the specific interface design details for the legacy MIS system migration? (Owned by PM/QA)
Who should receive follow-up notifications and what is the schedule for these notifications? (Owned by PM/QA)

decision_history: AI interpretation runs will create a background task with a completion notification upon completion.
Images for AI interpretation can originate from two sources: a one-time large-scale batch from Vendor A, or individual user uploads.
After AI interpretation, users can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch or monitoring.
Placing a suspected point under monitoring requires providing a reason and uploading a polygon (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out).
A mechanism is required to prevent duplicate image uploads for large files.
The two-period image comparison feature will no longer pull from an image library; users will annotate this manually.
The map viewer must be capable of loading historically uploaded imagery and support filtering by date, image-name keyword (type: satellite, aerial photo, drone), and displaying points on the map.
When a point is opened in the map viewer, a 'locate' button will be available to automatically zoom the map to that location.
If the map is zoomed out beyond a specific scale, displayed imagery will automatically revert to a point display.
In comparison mode, the map view will be locked from panning; a 'release drag mode' button will be provided to restore panning.
Users must be able to adjust image transparency, which is important for orthophoto analysis.
Projects will be organized by year, and suspected points can be created within these yearly projects.
Each year will form one distinct suspected point layer.
Suspected points require the upload of a polygon, but no image upload is needed.
A 'New Survey Point Data' interface needs to be added, including fields for drawing number and key-monitoring-zone (referencing manual figure 2.13).

The existing legacy MIS file management system will be migrated to the new system; its interface will be redesigned, but core functionality will remain unchanged. This migration is driven by information-security compliance requirements.
For the new system's follow-up feature, items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module.
The new system's follow-up feature will include a schedule-tracking interface (e.g., showing days remaining).
The new system will include a follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien has already prepared a first draft).
After a follow-up is completed, it will connect to survey-report management, including recording time, description, action taken, and uploaded images.
The data flow documentation will cover the path from the image source, through interpretation task execution (at the workstation), to result output, and finally to the monitoring database.
dependencies: An on-site workstation must be procured and deployed for AI model execution, with images sent to the client's Access Point (AP) which then calls the workstation to run the model.
The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer; integration with this provider and GeoServer setup is required.
The map viewer's design will reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System; access to and analysis of this system is required.
A-Bao has requested a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation (assigned to Sherry, due 2026-05-18).
Sherry must finish the 'add suspected point' data interface (referencing figure 2.13) by 2026-05-18.
Sherry must organize all existing interfaces (including those managed by Shao-Chien) for the requirements interview by 2026-05-20.
Preparation of a system architecture diagram is needed for the requirements interview.
Preparation of a data flow diagram is needed for the requirements interview.
risks: The project kick-off (expected mid-May) is contingent on a smooth bidding process. Any delays in the bidding process will directly impact the project timeline. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High)

The immediate requirements interview and interface confirmation on the afternoon of the bid award day (D032) poses a risk of an unproductive or delayed start if preparation (consolidating interfaces, preparing architecture and data flow diagrams) is not completed on time. (Likelihood: Medium, Impact: High)
open_questions: High Impact: What are the specific details regarding the notification mechanism for AI interpretation task completion (e.g., notification channels, content, configuration options)? (Owned by PM/QA)
High Impact: Should suspected-point layers be split by county/city? This decision impacts data structure and user interface design. (Owned by PM/Architect)
Medium Impact: What are the specific interface design details for the legacy MIS system migration? Confirmation is needed to meet user expectations and functional requirements. (Owned by PM/UX)
Medium Impact: Who are the recipients of follow-up notifications, and what is the desired schedule for these notifications? (Owned by PM/QA)
milestones: Project expected to kick off in mid-May, contingent on a smooth bidding process.
Requirements interview and interface confirmation to happen immediately on the afternoon of the bid award day.
Next meeting scheduled for 2026-05-15 to discuss system architecture.
Sherry to complete the 'add suspected point' data interface (referencing figure 2.13) by 2026-05-18.
Sherry to prepare the data-flow document for A-Bao's review by 2026-05-18.
Sherry to organize all existing interfaces (including Shao-Chien's parts) for the requirements interview by 2026-05-20.

system_requirements: The system SHALL create a single background task for each AI interpretation run (D001).
The system SHALL send a notification upon the completion of an AI interpretation task (D001).
The system SHALL support two sources for AI interpretation images: a one-time large-scale batch from Vendor A, and individual user-uploaded images (D003).
Upon completion of AI interpretation, the system SHALL generate a list of suspected points (D004).
The system SHALL allow users to confirm (yes/no) suspected points (D004).
The system SHALL allow users to mark confirmed suspected points for watch or monitoring (D004).
When a user places a suspected point under monitoring, the system SHALL require the user to provide a reason (D005).
The system SHALL require the user to upload a polygon when placing a suspected point under monitoring (D005).
The system SHALL automatically filter out overly small polygons provided for monitoring suspected points (D005).
The system SHALL implement a mechanism to prevent duplicate image uploads (D006).
The system SHALL enable users to manually annotate images for two-period image comparison (D007).
The system MUST NOT automatically pull images from the image library for two-period image comparison (D007).
The map viewer SHALL be capable of loading historically uploaded imagery (D010).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering the imagery list by date (D010).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering the imagery list by image-name keyword (D010).
The map viewer SHALL support filtering imagery by type (satellite, aerial photo, drone) (D010).
The map viewer SHALL display points on the map (D010).
The map viewer SHALL provide a 'locate' button when a point is opened (D011).
Upon activation of the 'locate' button, the map viewer SHALL automatically zoom to the specific location of the point (D011).
The map viewer SHALL automatically revert imagery display to point display when zoomed out beyond a specific scale (D012).
In comparison mode, the map viewer SHALL lock the map view, preventing panning (D013).
The map viewer SHALL provide a 'release drag mode' button to restore panning functionality in comparison mode (D013).
The map viewer SHALL allow users to adjust image transparency (D014).
The system SHALL organize projects by year (D016).
The system SHALL allow the creation of suspected points within yearly projects (D016).
The system SHALL require an uploaded polygon for suspected points (D018).
The system MUST NOT require an image upload for suspected points (D018).
For suspected point management, each year SHALL constitute a distinct layer (D019).
The migrated legacy MIS file management system SHALL comply with information-security requirements (inferred - D022).
The new system's follow-up/reminder feature SHALL incorporate email notifications as a primary mechanism, similar to the legacy system (inferred - D024).
The system SHALL allow selection of suspected points as items for follow-up (D025).
The system SHALL integrate selected suspected points into the follow-up module (D025).
The system SHALL include a schedule-tracking interface within the follow-up feature, displaying information such as days remaining (D026).
The system SHALL include a follow-up dispatch interface (D027).
Upon completion of a follow-up, the system SHALL connect to survey-report management (D028).
The system SHALL record follow-up time, description, action taken, and uploaded images within survey-report management (D028).
data_flows: Image source (Vendor A batch or User Upload) -> Client's Access Point (AP) -> On-site workstation (for AI interpretation task execution) -> Result output -> Monitoring database (D030).
interface_contracts: A 'New Survey Point Data' interface SHALL be added, including fields for 'drawing number' and 'key-monitoring-zone' (reference manual figure 2.13 for specific details) (D020).

constraints.text: The AI model execution SHALL occur on an on-site workstation (D008). Images SHALL be sent to the client's Access Point (AP), which SHALL then initiate the workstation to run the model (D008).
The system SHALL use GIS Provider B as the source for the base map, and the base map SHALL be served via GeoServer (D009).

architectural_decisions.text: The map viewer design SHALL reference the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System (D015).
The legacy MIS file management system SHALL be migrated to the new system; its interface will be redesigned but core functionality will remain unchanged (D021).

open_analysis_questions: What are the specific details of the AI interpretation task completion notification mechanism (e.g., type, content, delivery method)? (D002, D041)
Should suspected point layers be segmented by county/city? (D017, D042)
What are the specific interface design details for the legacy MIS system migration? (D023, D043)
Who are the recipients of follow-up notifications, and what is their corresponding schedule? (D044)

stakeholder.business_summary: This project is currently in the bidding phase and is expected to kick off in mid-May. The core focus is on developing a new system for AI-powered image interpretation and map viewing, alongside migrating our existing legacy data management system to enhance information security. We've confirmed several key functionalities and user experience improvements, but still need to finalize some details with stakeholders.
key_impact: Improved efficiency in identifying areas of interest through AI-powered image analysis, with clear tracking of results.
Enhanced ability to manage and review historical imagery and suspected points, allowing for better decision-making and monitoring.
Modernized user experience with a redesigned map viewer and streamlined workflows for managing survey data and follow-ups.
Ensured compliance with information security standards by migrating the legacy data management system.
Better oversight and accountability with integrated follow-up tracking and reporting.
decisions_needing_alignment: We need to define the specific mechanism for notifying users when AI image analysis tasks are complete.
Confirmation is required on whether suspected points (areas of interest) should be organized and displayed separately by county or city.
The exact design details for the user interfaces of the migrated legacy file management system need to be confirmed.
We need to determine who will receive follow-up notifications and on what schedule.
major_unresolved_questions: What is the exact process and user experience for receiving notifications once AI image interpretation is finished?
How should we best organize suspected points on the map – should they be categorized by county or city for easier management?
What will the new user interface for the migrated legacy data system look like, and how will it function to ensure it meets user needs?
Who specifically needs to be informed about follow-up actions and when should these reminders be sent?

issues.description: The claim that 'The new system's follow-up/reminder feature SHALL incorporate email notifications as a primary mechanism, similar to the legacy system' is an overstatement and a hallucination. Decision D024 only states that the *legacy* system uses email notifications. Decision D044 explicitly lists 'Who are the recipients of follow-up notifications and their corresponding schedule' as an open question needing confirmation. Presenting this as a 'SHALL' requirement for the new system contradicts the open question and overstates certainty.
issues.suggestion: Remove this inferred requirement from the 'system_requirements' section. It is currently an open question and should not be stated as a confirmed requirement or a 'SHALL' statement. The open question D044 already addresses this uncertainty.

review.reviewer_notes: The report generally maintains good quality across all role views, with claims traceable to extracted decisions. However, a critical hallucination was identified in the Systems Analysis view regarding the follow-up notification mechanism, which contradicts an explicitly open question. This issue needs to be resolved before publication.

source_map.decision_title: AI interpretation run creates background task with notification
evidence_quotes: Each AI interpretation run creates one task, which runs in the background in the task list (similar to how the CC platform operates); a notification is sent when it completes

source_map.decision_title: AI interpretation notification mechanism details pending
evidence_quotes: Notification mechanism details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

source_map.decision_title: Two image sources for AI interpretation
evidence_quotes: Two image sources: a one-time large-scale nationwide batch of imagery obtained from Vendor A, or individual images uploaded by the user

source_map.decision_title: User confirmation and monitoring of suspected points
evidence_quotes: After interpretation completes, a list is generated; the user can confirm suspected points (yes/no) and decide whether to place them under watch/monitoring

source_map.decision_title: Monitoring suspected points requires reason and polygon upload
evidence_quotes: When placing a point under monitoring, the reason must be filled in and a polygon uploaded (large-area polygons are valid; overly small areas are automatically filtered out)

source_map.decision_title: Duplicate image upload prevention for large files
evidence_quotes: Images must not be uploaded more than once (image files are large, so a duplicate-prevention mechanism is needed)

source_map.decision_title: Two-period image comparison will be manual annotation
evidence_quotes: Two-period image comparison will no longer pull from the image library — the user will annotate this manually instead

source_map.decision_title: On-site workstation for model execution
evidence_quotes: A workstation will be set up on-site; images are sent to the client's AP, which then calls the workstation to run the model

source_map.decision_title: Base map provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer
evidence_quotes: The base map will be provided by GIS Provider B via GeoServer

source_map.decision_title: Map viewer functionality for historical imagery and filtering
evidence_quotes: The map viewer can load historically uploaded imagery, supports filtering the list by date and image-name keyword (type: satellite / aerial photo / drone), and displays points on the map

source_map.decision_title: Map viewer 'locate' button for points
evidence_quotes: Opening a point shows a "locate" button; the map automatically zooms to that location

source_map.decision_title: Automatic imagery revert to point display on zoom out
evidence_quotes: When the map is zoomed out past a certain scale, imagery automatically reverts to point display

source_map.decision_title: Map view locked in comparison mode with release option
evidence_quotes: In comparison mode, the map view is locked and cannot be panned; a "release drag mode" button is provided to restore panning

source_map.decision_title: User adjustable image transparency
evidence_quotes: Users can adjust image transparency (an important feature for orthophoto analysis)

source_map.decision_title: Map viewer design references Forest Land Disaster Prevention System
evidence_quotes: The map viewer's design references the existing Forest Land Disaster Prevention System

source_map.decision_title: Projects organized by year for suspected points
evidence_quotes: Projects are organized by year; suspected points can be created within a project

source_map.decision_title: Suspected point layers by county/city pending confirmation
evidence_quotes: Whether to split layers by county/city is pending confirmation in the requirements interview

source_map.decision_title: Suspected points require polygon, no image upload
evidence_quotes: Suspected points require an uploaded polygon; no image upload is needed

source_map.decision_title: Each year forms one suspected point layer
evidence_quotes: Each year forms one layer

source_map.decision_title: New Survey Point Data interface needed
evidence_quotes: Need to add a "New Survey Point Data" interface (including drawing number and key-monitoring-zone fields, refer to manual figure 2.13)

source_map.decision_title: Legacy MIS file system migration and redesign
evidence_quotes: The original MS file management system will be migrated to the new system; the interface will be redesigned but functionality will remain unchanged

source_map.decision_title: MIS migration driven by information-security compliance
evidence_quotes: The main reason for migration is information-security compliance requirements

source_map.decision_title: MIS migration details pending confirmation
evidence_quotes: Migration details are pending confirmation in the requirements interview

source_map.decision_title: Legacy system has email-based follow-up/reminder feature
evidence_quotes: The legacy system already has a follow-up/reminder feature, primarily via email notification

source_map.decision_title: New system follow-up from suspected points
evidence_quotes: Items to follow up on can be selected from suspected points and integrated into the follow-up module

source_map.decision_title: New system follow-up schedule tracking interface
evidence_quotes: Add a schedule-tracking interface (e.g. showing days remaining)

source_map.decision_title: New system follow-up dispatch interface
evidence_quotes: Follow-up dispatch interface (Shao-Chien already has a first draft)

source_map.decision_title: Follow-up connects to survey-report management
evidence_quotes: After follow-up, this connects to survey-report management: including time, description, action taken, and uploaded images

source_map.decision_title: A-Bao requests data-flow document for AI interpretation
evidence_quotes: A-Bao needs a data-flow document explaining how data moves after AI interpretation completes — where the data is pulled from, and where computation happens

source_map.decision_title: Data flow scope: image source to monitoring database
evidence_quotes: Data flow covers: image source → interpretation task execution (workstation) → result output → monitoring database

source_map.decision_title: Project bidding process underway, expected mid-May kick-off
evidence_quotes: The project is currently in the bidding process; if things go smoothly, it's expected to kick off in mid-May

source_map.decision_title: Requirements interview and interface confirmation immediately post-bid award
evidence_quotes: On the afternoon of the day the bid is awarded, a requirements interview and interface confirmation may need to happen immediately

source_map.decision_title: Sherry to finish 'add suspected point' data interface by 2026-05-18
